title: Claude Code Ecosystem Level One Certification
headline: Go from getting started to intermediate, fast.
subheadline: Learn the workflows, prompting patterns, and execution habits that help operators, employees, and business owners save two to four hours a day once they put the training into practice.
audience: Founders, executives, and business owners who want to learn AI
Employees who want to level up their skills
Employers who want their teams trained without spending hours on redundant, pointless training
outcomes: Move from beginner-level usage to an intermediate working command of the ecosystem
Save two to four hours a day by applying the workflows taught in the certification
Get certified so you are better positioned to work with businesses using AI
Train teams faster without forcing them through bloated, repetitive onboarding
Understand how to use chat, workflow tools, code, and integrations together in one system

login.title: Get Free Access
login.subheadline: Enter your email to unlock the certification portal and opt into the newsletter if you want future updates and training drops.

modules.title: Install, Access, And Prompt The Right Way
modules.headline: Start with a clean account, a local app, and a voice-first prompt workflow.
modules.subheadline: Set up Claude Pro, download the desktop app, install Claude Code in the terminal, and use dictated prompts with simple delimiters so Claude returns a usable checklist instead of vague ideas.
focus: Account setup and the Pro-plan requirement for Claude Code
Desktop download plus terminal installation flow
Prompting by voice with WhisperFlow-style dictation
Using separators and examples to make prompts more reliable

modules.title: Map The Claude Ecosystem
modules.headline: Understand where Chat, Cowork, and Code each fit before you try to operationalize them.
modules.subheadline: Get a fast product tour of the three working modes, including how Cowork handles business workflows, scheduling, and connectors while Code handles the deeper build work.
focus: The role of Chat for quick questions and one-off answers
The role of Cowork for connected business workflows
The role of Code for deeper execution and build tasks
A quick look at customization, scheduling, and connected apps

modules.title: Configure For Speed Without Losing Control
modules.headline: Remove the friction that slows teams down, but understand where the sharp edges are.
modules.subheadline: Enable bypass permissions mode responsibly, turn on browser access, choose the right working folder, and decide when auto-accept helps versus when it creates unnecessary risk.
focus: Bypass permissions mode and when it is worth enabling
Claude-in-browser access for logins and web actions
Project-folder discipline for reliable local work
A concrete warning about over-trusting destructive cleanup tasks

modules.title: Adopt The Orchestrator Mindset
modules.headline: Stop acting like the person doing every task and start managing teams of agents.
modules.subheadline: Use Claude-generated checklists as a starting point, refine vague prompts into role-based work, and learn how experienced operators juggle multiple tabs and terminals while Claude handles execution.
focus: Thinking in terms of orchestration instead of manual execution
Letting Claude produce the first draft of the plan
Refining prompts when outputs are too personal or too vague
Turning one prompt into role-based demos for recruiting, ops, and software

modules.title: Reinforce The Business Workflow Layer
modules.headline: Revisit the Cowork surface so non-technical users do not get lost.
modules.subheadline: Use this shorter segment as a quick orientation pass through the business-facing side of Claude, including connected apps, schedules, and the working area where recurring tasks get managed.
focus: A fast refresher on the Cowork interface
Connected apps and schedule-based workflows
Repetition that helps non-technical viewers retain the product map

modules.title: Prompt From The Role, The Stack, And The Outcome
modules.headline: The best prompts sound like a real operator describing a real mess that needs to be cleaned up.
modules.subheadline: Watch an operations scenario get framed in plain English around ClickUp, CRM, Gmail, Calendar, and a local dashboard so Claude can recommend the right setup without requiring the user to be technical.
focus: Using current tools and pain points as prompt inputs
Asking for a single home base across ClickUp, CRM, email, and calendar
Stating technical limits clearly so Claude adapts to the user

modules.title: Ask For The Right Integration Layer
modules.headline: When a connector is not obvious, the vocabulary matters.
modules.subheadline: Learn how to ask Claude about API-based integrations, MCPs, skills, and CLI routes so the platform can tell you what is available and what kind of access it needs next.
focus: Why API keys are not the only integration path
How MCPs, skills, and CLI options expand what Claude can do
How to ask better follow-up questions when a system is not prebuilt
